jp_disassembly_reference.evidence: The sibling Japanese disassembly uses the same BPEJ baserom hash and confirms the JP build contains the standard m4a/sound code paths. Its current map/cfg do not preserve split song/sample labels, so each manifest item is additionally limited to ROM offsets whose bytes match the current named Expansion asset.

song_table.evidence: The table is the only BPEJ structure found with the expected Emerald gSongTable entry pattern: mus_dummy followed by SE entries, valid song pointers, and music player ids 0/1/1.

songs.label: mus_dummy
songs.byte_match_detail: BPEJ song header bytes match the current mus_dummy header.
songs.evidence: BPEJ song table index 0 points to this header; it is the dummy BGM entry.
